356.name: INR
356.scamNote: Fraudsters paste their own UPI QR sticker over a shop's genuine one, or ask victims to "scan to receive" a refund — which is never how receiving money works.

986.name: BRL
986.scamNote: Brazilian fraudsters have re-broadcast legitimate livestreams (e.g. charity drives) with a swapped Pix QR code overlaid, redirecting donations to themselves.

458.name: MYR
458.scamNote: Malaysian authorities have documented stalls and restaurants where the genuine DuitNow QR sticker was physically replaced with a fraudster's own code.

360.name: IDR
360.scamNote: Indonesian police have reported QRIS stickers swapped at mosques, parking stands, and small merchants, redirecting donations or payments to a scammer's account.

704.name: VND
704.scamNote: Reported VietQR scams include fake "loan approval" or "refund" codes sent over Zalo/Facebook that require scanning to supposedly receive money.

608.name: PHP
608.scamNote: Reported QR Ph scams include fake delivery-fee or rider "cash-in" QR codes presented by impersonators posing as couriers or vendors.

764.name: THB
764.scamNote: Reported PromptPay scams include fraudulent QR stickers placed over donation boxes and market-stall payment signs.

702.name: SGD
702.scamNote: Singapore police have warned of PayNow QR codes shared in messaging apps for fake "prize claim" or "deposit refund" scams.

116.name: KHR
116.scamNote: Sticker-swap scams targeting merchant payment QR codes have been reported across the region — confirm the payee name shown in your banking app matches the merchant.

144.name: LKR
144.scamNote: Sticker-swap scams targeting merchant payment QR codes have been reported across the region — confirm the payee name shown in your banking app matches the merchant.
